description: SCARA Robot Axis Configuration - Translation from physical units to motor steps

robot_geometry.comment: L1 = shoulder to elbow (X axis) = 228mm, L2 = elbow to end effector (Z axis) = 144.5mm (from original HowToMechatronics design)
robot_geometry.coordinate_system: Origin at shoulder rotation point (base). +X = user's right, +Y = toward user (away from robot base).

Y.name: Y Axis
Y.comment_speed: Default speed in steps per second

calibration_markers.description: ArUco marker positions in robot coordinate system (cm) - Origin at Marker 0
calibration_markers.coordinate_system: Origin at Marker 0. +X and +Y directions based on relative marker positions

detection_settings.description: Color detection region of interest (ROI) settings

color_detection_roi.description: ROI for color object detection (x, y, width, height) in camera pixels. Adjust to match your workspace area. Default: 50px margin from 640x480 camera

calibration_notes.steps_per_mm: For linear axes with GT2 belt (2mm pitch) and 20-tooth pulley: steps_per_mm = (200 * 16) / (2 * 20) = 160
calibration_notes.steps_per_degree: For direct drive 200-step motor with 16x microstepping: steps_per_degree = (200 * 16) / 360 = 8.889
calibration_notes.microstepping: Update values based on your A4988 driver microstepping configuration (usually 1, 2, 4, 8, or 16)
calibration_notes.direction: If axis moves in wrong direction, change default_direction from 1 to -1 or vice versa
